title: Interpret Container Selection Screen Fields For Tote And Location Information
summary: Use the Container Selection screen to identify the displayed container or tote ID, associated location information, and site-relevant meanings of fields shown in the training source. The training explains that the green number is the container or tote ID, the Location field shows associated rack or AGV location information when displayed, Layer Count reflects the rack configuration and is one at this site, Logic ID is present but not used in this deployment, and exception-related fields may appear as interface features that this site does not use.
when_to_use: Use when viewing the Container Selection screen and needing to interpret the displayed container or tote ID, location-related information, Layer Count, Logic ID, or exception-related fields according to this training source.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to assign operational meaning to Logic ID beyond the source statement that it is not used in this environment.

Do not use this runbook to interpret exception box or abnormal-related fields as active site workflows beyond the source statement that these features are available in the interface but not used at this site.
Do not use this runbook if the displayed fields do not match the documented training explanation.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: View the Container Selection screen and interpret displayed fields using the training-backed meanings in this runbook.

safety_notes: Use only the field meanings explicitly supported by the training source.
Do not assign additional operational meaning to Logic ID or exception-related fields beyond what the source states.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Container Selection screen
Visual access to the tote label if cross-checking the tote ID

access_required: Access to the Container Selection screen
system_preconditions: The Container Selection screen is open or visible.
A container, tote, rack, or AGV-related record is displayed on the screen.

visual_references.description: Training frame showing the Container Selection screen with container field, location field, Layer Count, Logic ID, and related interface fields.

steps.title: Open or view the Container Selection screen
steps.purpose: Establish the correct screen for interpreting tote and location-related fields.
steps.instruction: Open or view the Container Selection screen and visually confirm that the screen includes the container-related fields described in the training, including the container field and the location-related fields shown on the training slide.
steps.expected_result: The Container Selection screen is visible and the relevant fields can be reviewed.
steps.healthy_condition: The screen matches the training view and shows the expected container-related information fields.
steps.failure_condition: The screen is not available, does not match the training view, or the displayed fields cannot be aligned to the source-backed labels.

actions.label: View Container Selection screen
actions.exact_action: Open or view the Container Selection screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Container Selection screen showing the container field, location field, Layer Count, Logic ID, and right-side interface fields.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed screen does not match the documented Container Selection training view.
The fields needed for interpretation are not visible or cannot be identified from the source-backed screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR shows a Container Selection screen with Location, Layer Count, Logic ID, and Container fields.
Training slide showing the container selection view for totes, including container metadata fields.

steps.title: Identify the green number as the container or tote ID
steps.purpose: Interpret the primary identifier shown for the selected item.
steps.instruction: Identify the green number shown for the selected item and interpret it as the container or tote ID. If needed, cross-check it against the tote label, which the training says is also visible on the tote.
steps.expected_result: The displayed green number is recognized as the container or tote ID for the selected item.
steps.healthy_condition: The green number can be read and consistently interpreted as the container or tote ID.
steps.failure_condition: The green number is missing, unclear, or cannot be reconciled with the source explanation.

actions.label: Read green identifier
actions.exact_action: Identify the green number shown for the selected item and interpret it as the container or tote ID.

actions.label: Cross-check tote label if needed
actions.exact_action: Cross-check the displayed ID against the tote label if visual access to the tote label is available.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The green number on the Container Selection screen that the training identifies as the container or tote ID.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed identifier cannot be interpreted as the container or tote ID from the source-backed screen.
The displayed identifier conflicts with the training explanation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The 113 in green is the container or total ID.
Transcript states that the green number is the container and total number/ID, and that tote labels are visible on totes.

steps.title: Check the Location field for associated rack or AGV location
steps.purpose: Determine where the selected container or tote is associated in the system.
steps.instruction: Check the Location field for the associated rack or AGV location if it is displayed on the screen.
steps.expected_result: The associated location information is identified from the Location field when present.
steps.healthy_condition: A location value is visible and can be interpreted as the associated rack or AGV location.
steps.failure_condition: The Location field is absent, unclear, or cannot be interpreted from the source-backed screen.

actions.label: Review Location field
actions.exact_action: Check the Location field for the associated rack or AGV location if it is displayed on the screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Location field on the Container Selection screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Location field does not appear when expected.
The displayed location information cannot be interpreted from the source-backed labels.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR shows a Container Selection screen with Location, Layer Count, Logic ID, and Container fields.
Container Selection screen for tote or container lookup. Shows container ID, location, layer count, logic ID, and rack or AGV association.

steps.title: Interpret Layer Count using the site single-layer note
steps.purpose: Apply the training-specific meaning of Layer Count for this deployment.
steps.instruction: Review the Layer Count field and interpret it using the site note that this deployment uses single-density racks with one layer.
steps.expected_result: Layer Count is understood as reflecting the rack configuration and is interpreted as one for this site.
steps.healthy_condition: Layer Count is read in a way that matches the training statement that the site uses one layer.
steps.failure_condition: Layer Count appears inconsistent with the training explanation or cannot be interpreted from the source.

actions.label: Review Layer Count
actions.exact_action: Review the Layer Count field and interpret it using the site note that this deployment uses single-density racks with one layer.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Layer Count field on the Container Selection screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Layer Count does not align with the training statement that this site uses one layer.
The field meaning cannot be confirmed from the source-backed explanation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Layer Count on the screen reflects rack configuration. In this training source, racks are single density with one layer, so Layer Count is expected to show one.
All shelves or racks are single density, so only one layer is used; Layer Count is tied to the rack property and is one for this site.

steps.title: Treat Logic ID as present but unused in this environment
steps.purpose: Prevent unsupported interpretation of a field that appears on the screen but is not used at this site.
steps.instruction: Note that the Logic ID field may appear on the screen, but interpret it only as a displayed field that the training states is not used in this environment.
steps.expected_result: Logic ID is recognized as present on the interface without assigning unsupported operational meaning to it.
steps.healthy_condition: The field is acknowledged but not used for site interpretation beyond the source statement.
steps.failure_condition: Additional meaning is assigned to Logic ID beyond what the source supports.

actions.label: Review Logic ID field
actions.exact_action: Note that the Logic ID field may appear on the screen but the training states it is not used in this environment.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Logic ID field on the Container Selection screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The screen behavior suggests Logic ID is being used in a way that conflicts with the training explanation.
The field cannot be matched to the source-backed screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Logic ID appears on the container-related screen, but the training says this field is not used in this deployment.
The training identifies Logic ID as one of the displayed fields and states that they do not use the Logic ID.

steps.title: Recognize exception-related fields as available but not used at this site
steps.purpose: Clarify the scope of right-side exception-related interface fields shown in the training.
steps.instruction: If exception-related fields such as exception box or abnormal-related fields appear on the right side, recognize them as available interface features that the training says this site does not use.
steps.expected_result: Exception-related fields are recognized as present interface features without being treated as active site workflow elements.
steps.healthy_condition: The user understands that these fields may appear but are not used in this implementation according to the source.
steps.failure_condition: The user assigns unsupported site workflow meaning to exception-related fields.

actions.label: Review exception-related fields
actions.exact_action: If exception-related fields such as exception box or abnormal-related fields appear on the right side, recognize them as available interface features that the training says this site does not use.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Right-side exception box and abnormal-related fields on the Container Selection screen, if visible.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed fields do not match the training explanation of available but unused features.
The interface presents exception-related fields that cannot be interpreted from the source-backed labels.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Exception box and abnormal-related fields appear on the screen, but the training says these are broader Geek Plus features not used in this site's implementation.
Exception box and abnormal-related fields are mentioned as Geek Plus features that this site has not had to use.

success_criteria: The user can identify the Container Selection screen and its relevant fields.
The green number is correctly interpreted as the container or tote ID.
The Location field is correctly recognized as the associated rack or AGV location when displayed.
Layer Count is interpreted according to the site note that the deployment uses one layer.
Logic ID is recognized as present but not used in this environment.
Exception-related fields are recognized as available interface features not used at this site.
healthy_conditions: The displayed screen matches the training-backed Container Selection view.
Field meanings align with the source explanation.
Only source-supported meanings are assigned to displayed fields.
failure_conditions: The displayed fields do not match the documented training explanation.
The screen cannot be interpreted from the source-backed labels.
Logic ID or exception-related fields are given unsupported operational meaning.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the displayed fields do not match the documented training explanation.
Escalate if the screen cannot be interpreted from the source-backed labels.
Escalate if field behavior appears inconsistent with the training statements about Layer Count, Logic ID, or exception-related fields.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall Container Selection screen with container ID, Location, Layer Count, Logic ID, and right-side exception-related fields.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The 113 in green is the container or total ID; OCR shows a Container Selection screen with Location, Layer Count, Logic ID, and Container fields.

missing_details: The source does not provide a time estimate for using this reference procedure.
The source does not define supporting roles beyond the operator-oriented training explanation.
The source does not provide explicit commands or system navigation clicks for opening the Container Selection screen in this segment.
The source transcript is partially noisy, so field meanings were limited to source-backed statements.
